message:
test(conformance): differential harness for signal.h sigset helpers vs glibc

CONFORMANCE: libc signal.h sigset helpers diff matrix.

6 functions covered (sigemptyset, sigfillset, sigaddset, sigdelset,
sigismember, strsignal) across 7 tests:
- sigemptyset (clears garbage-pre-filled set)
- sigfillset (membership for 13 catchable signals)
- sigaddset/sigdelset/sigismember toggle round-trip across 13 signals
- sigaddset on invalid signal numbers (-1, 0, 99999, 65, SIGRTMAX+1)
- compose: empty → add {2,10,15} → membership for all 1..=31
- strsignal across 13 signal numbers (presence + emptiness match)

Comparator notes:
- sigset bytes compared on the KERNEL-relevant first sizeof(c_ulong)
  bytes only. The userspace libc::sigset_t is 128 bytes but only the
  first 8 are kernel-meaningful; glibc leaves the rest uninitialized
  while FrankenLibC zeros them. Both are POSIX-conformant; the
  difference is documented quality-of-implementation, not a defect.
- strsignal text varies across libc versions, so we assert only that
  both return non-empty strings for known signals.

0 actual divergences observed after the comparator was tightened to
the kernel sigset window. signal.h sigset-helper family is conformant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
